message:
docs(profiling): update pane hotspot capture methodology

Extend hotspot capture documentation with additional profiling insights.
Sync beads tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1xhje","title":"Inline present cleanup on I/O error","description":"TerminalWriter::present_inline uses ? on writes; on IO error it returns before restoring cursor/sync/scroll-region, leaving the terminal in a bad state if the runtime keeps going. Add best-effort cleanup on error.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TurquoiseBeacon","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:49:38.633291975Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:06:18.594195276Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:06:18.594177763Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: present_inline wraps errors and calls best_effort_inline_cleanup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1xo","title":"Windows Terminal compatibility layer","description":"PHASE 7 - POLISH: WINDOWS SUPPORT

## Background
Windows Terminal v1 support is deferred but must have clear integration path. Modern Windows Terminal supports ANSI, older conhost needs different handling.

## Technical Requirements (v1 Scope - Windows Terminal Only)
- Detect Windows Terminal vs legacy conhost
- Enable VT processing mode via SetConsoleMode
- Handle Windows-specific escape sequences
- PTY spawning via ConPTY API
- Signal handling differences (Ctrl+C, Ctrl+Break)
- Path handling for Windows conventions

## Out of Scope for v1
- Legacy conhost support (Windows 7/8)
- PowerShell-specific handling
- Windows-specific widgets

## Design Rationale
Per ADR-004, Windows v1 targets modern Windows Terminal only. This dramatically simplifies implementation while covering majority of Windows developer use.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Windows Terminal detection
- [ ] VT mode enabling
- [ ] ConPTY integration for PTY tests
- [ ] Signal handling shim
- [ ] CI testing on Windows
- [ ] Feature gate: windows

## Testing
- GitHub Actions Windows runner
- Manual testing on Windows Terminal
- Document known limitations

## Location: ftui-render crate + ftui-pty crate (platform-specific code)","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:20:16.533908639Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:01:10.730619535Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:01:10.730598736Z","close_reason":"Windows Terminal detection: treat WT_SESSION as non-dumb even when TERM is empty; added unit test + docs update.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xo","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.10.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1xo","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

optimization target.\n\nAcceptance\n- Top bottlenecks are identified with evidence.\n- Optimization backlog is prioritized by measurable impact.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Implement scoped performance work based on measured baselines/profiles.\n2) Add benchmark or regression checks aligned with the affected hotspot/budget.\n3) Verify behavior equivalence against golden outputs where optimizations apply.\n4) Emit detailed timing/allocation diagnostics sufficient for CI and local triage.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-11T05:34:26.842109688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T18:44:07.046352143Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["hotspots","pane","perf","profiling"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-23pre","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-2bav7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-2dy6m","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-2eqqy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","depends_on_id":"bd-3jmx7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1057,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Profiling conclusion summary for the next optimization phase: the pane-core replay path is not a good candidate for more micro-optimizations alone. The high-EV opportunities are architectural: (1) stop replaying from the baseline for deep histories, (2) stop paying whole-tree clone/validate costs for local operations, (3) improve profiling fidelity with longer-lived harnesses and symbolized artifacts, and (4) make memory retention explicit and bounded for checkpointing/persistent structures. The new child/dependent bead graph created from this comment is intended to be fully self-contained so future sessions can work directly from Beads without consulting any external optimization memo.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:33:45Z"},{"id":1082,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Progress update: started a profiling-evidence cleanup slice. `scripts/pane_profile.sh` now emits `symbol_metadata.txt` for the three pane bench binaries, recording binary paths plus debug-info readiness, and `docs/profiling/bd-1y0ph-pane-hotspot-capture.md` has been updated so the note no longer incorrectly claims the bench binaries are still stripped. This tightens the profiling bundle itself so future `perf report` work can trust symbol-readiness from captured artifacts rather than assumption.","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:44:07Z"}]}

conclusion summary for the next optimization phase: the pane-core replay path is not a good candidate for more micro-optimizations alone. The high-EV opportunities are architectural: (1) stop replaying from the baseline for deep histories, (2) stop paying whole-tree clone/validate costs for local operations, (3) improve profiling fidelity with longer-lived harnesses and symbolized artifacts, and (4) make memory retention explicit and bounded for checkpointing/persistent structures. The new child/dependent bead graph created from this comment is intended to be fully self-contained so future sessions can work directly from Beads without consulting any external optimization memo.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:33:45Z"},{"id":1082,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Progress update: started a profiling-evidence cleanup slice. `scripts/pane_profile.sh` now emits `symbol_metadata.txt` for the three pane bench binaries, recording binary paths plus debug-info readiness, and `docs/profiling/bd-1y0ph-pane-hotspot-capture.md` has been updated so the note no longer incorrectly claims the bench binaries are still stripped. This tightens the profiling bundle itself so future `perf report` work can trust symbol-readiness from captured artifacts rather than assumption.","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:44:07Z"},{"id":1083,"issue_id":"bd-1y0ph","author":"AmberBadger","text":"Progress update: reran `./scripts/pane_profile.sh --test` after fixing the symbol-metadata path selection bug. Current artifact truth is now explicit: `layout_bench` reports `binary_source=executed` and `debug_info=present`, while `pane_terminal_bench` and `pane_pointer_bench` report `binary_source=executed` but `local_binary_status=missing` after `rch` artifact retrieval. This means the profiling bundle is now honest about what can and cannot support local `perf report` stack attribution; the remaining blocker for fully symbolized multi-surface profiling is preserving or retrieving the exact runtime/web bench binaries locally.","created_at":"2026-03-14T21:33:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1y98h","title":"Mermaid Error Rendering + Recovery Panel","description":"Background
- Parse/layout errors must be surfaced without breaking the UI.

Scope
- Define structured error panel for Mermaid blocks (header + location + message).
- Include fallback raw code view with line numbers when in debug mode.
- Support partial-render overlay when a recoverable AST exists.
- Honor config error_mode (panel/raw/both) and log decisions to JSONL.
- Ensure errors are deterministic and logged to JSONL with codes.

Considerations
- Errors must not crash rendering or contaminate layout caches.
- Keep panel width-aware with proper wrapping.

Success Criteria
- Invalid Mermaid never crashes; a readable error panel is shown.
- Errors include line/column and hint when possible.
- Snapshot tests for error panel rendering.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"HazyPuma","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:50:44.066534801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:30:14.175965777Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T08:30:14.175945789Z","close_reason":"Implemented error panel/overlay + raw view, added error codes + JSONL logging + snapshots","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1y98h","depends_on_id":"bd-12o4t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y98h","depends_on_id":"bd-315zw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1y98h","depends_on_id":"bd-pt0dv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1yba2","title":"Deep audit: fellow-agent code review and root-cause fixes","description":"Wide-net review of multi-agent modifications for bugs, reliability issues, and regressions; implement root-cause fixes and validate with rch-offloaded checks.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:35:14.433286252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T23:20:45.103381157Z","closed_at":"2026-02-24T23:20:45.103357693Z","close_reason":"Completed deep cross-agent audit pass with root-cause fixes and green gates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","cross-agent","stability"]}
 {"id":"bd-1ybk8","title":"E2E: Alt-screen + Inline regression sweep (core screens)","description":"Scope: Alt-screen + inline regression sweep across core screens (Dashboard, DataViz, LayoutLab, TerminalCapabilities, Markdown, WidgetGallery, VisualEffects). Capture hashes and timing for each screen/size.

Logging: JSONL per screen with hash, dims, render time, mode, and seed.

Considerations: Deterministic seeds; avoid flakiness; prioritize large screens (200x50) while still covering 80x24 and 120x40.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Sweep covers alt + inline modes for 80x24, 120x40, 200x50.
2) JSONL logs include screen, hash, dims, mode, and timing.
3) Hashes stable across runs with fixed seeds.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:14:54.974028830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T02:29:15.391801467Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T02:29:15.391778443Z","close_reason":"Sweep script updated + hashes logged; alt+inline sweep passed (80x24/120x40/200x50).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ybk8","depends_on_id":"bd-22n80","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ybk8","depends_on_id":"bd-3isi7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:31Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":284,"issue_id":"bd-1ybk8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Updated demo_showcase_screen_sweep_e2e.sh: default include 200x50, add --no-large, per-screen hash JSONL asserts, and force E2E_JSONL_FILE to run-local path. Ran sweep: 228/228 pass (alt+inline, 80x24/120x40/200x50). Log dir: /tmp/ftui_demo_sweep_det_0_15_det_0 (JSONL: demo_showcase_screen_sweep.jsonl).","created_at":"2026-02-05T02:28:03Z"}]}

@@ -128,7 +128,7 @@ matching binary happens to exist under `target/release/deps/`.
 
 Current evidence from that artifact is mixed:
 
-- `layout_bench`: `with debug_info, not stripped`
+- `layout_bench`: `binary_source=executed`, `with debug_info, not stripped`
 - `pane_terminal_bench`: exact executed binary path captured, but local binary
   currently missing after `rch` artifact retrieval
 - `pane_pointer_bench`: exact executed binary path captured, but local binary
@@ -139,3 +139,11 @@ terminal/web benches still need follow-up if we want uniformly trustworthy
 `perf report` stack attribution across all pane surfaces from the local artifact
 bundle. The immediate value of the new metadata is that it distinguishes
 "missing exact executed binary" from "found a different local binary and guessed."
+
+Latest verified `symbol_metadata.txt` state:
+
+```text
+layout_bench: binary_source=executed, debug_info=present
+pane_terminal_bench: binary_source=executed, local_binary_status=missing
+pane_pointer_bench: binary_source=executed, local_binary_status=missing
+```
